bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check) | Also: setThumbnailRef(values.thumbnail ?? null);

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: superRefinement tried to index a typed object by string — TS7053 'Element implicitly has an any type'
bugs.root_cause: The thumbnailRefinement function used `data[k]` with a string `k`, but `data` had no index signature. The compiler couldn't narrow the access.
bugs.fix: Replaced `data[k]` / `present.includes(k)` style with explicit per-key checks (if data.thumbnailContentType !== undefined → push('thumbnailContentType') …). No string indexing, no `any`.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: StubVideo type missing s3KeyThumbnail + thumbnailContentType after Video model gained the columns
bugs.root_cause: Adding nullable columns to the Video Prisma model widened the toVideoDto input type, which the StubVideo type alias used to declare mock prisma rows. The alias needed matching optional/nullable fields.
bugs.fix: Added `s3KeyThumbnail: string | null` + `thumbnailContentType: string | null` to StubVideo and stubCreatedVideo (both null — the existing tests don't exercise the thumbnail path).

bugs.error_message: `/dashboard/published` privacy segmented control (Public/Unlisted/Private) and date-range select appeared to do nothing — list always returned the same results.
bugs.root_cause: The `listPublishedVideos()` method built a `URLSearchParams` from `params.q / .page / .pageSize` only — `params.privacy` and `params.since` were silently dropped. The server schema (`listPublishedVideosQuerySchema`) and the types (`ListPublishedVideosParams`) supported both; the hook (`useListPublishedVideos`) forwarded them; the UI (`PrivacySegmented` + date Select) emitted them. Only the wire-layer query-string assembly was missing, so the server always saw an empty filter.
bugs.fix: Added `if (params?.privacy) search.set('privacy', params.privacy);` and `if (params?.since) search.set('since', params.since);` next to the existing `q`/`page`/`pageSize` lines in `listPublishedVideos()`. The server schema accepts 'all' and transforms it to undefined, so passing the raw value is fine.

bugs.error_message: In-memory cache limited to single-process; wanted Redis without touching the 4 service call sites + 1 test mock.
bugs.root_cause: cache singleton was eagerly instantiated as `new InMemoryCache()` at module load with no env-awareness. No way to inject Redis at boot.
bugs.fix: Refactored cache.ts: kept CacheClient interface + InMemoryCache class; added RedisCacheClient (ioredis lazyConnect + PING); replaced eager `cache` singleton with a delegating object whose methods forward to whichever backend initCache(env) picked. Added initCache(env) / verifyCache(env) / async disposeCache(). Boot-time service checks (Database + Cache + Queue) wired into apps/api/src/index.ts and apps/worker/src/index.ts before listen(), with ✓/✗ banner + hard-fail on required-service outage. Production-aware warnings in env.ts when REDIS_URL is unset.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: ffmpeg should be ffmpegCmd
Container clipflow-worker in restart loop: `Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'ioredis' imported from /app/apps/worker/dist/index.js`
bugs.root_cause: pnpm uses isolated mode by default — each workspace package's direct deps live under apps/<pkg>/node_modules/<dep> as relative symlinks into .pnpm/. The Dockerfile's runtime stage copied /app/node_modules but not /app/apps/worker/node_modules, so the worker's direct `import { Redis } from "ioredis"` could not resolve (the .pnpm/ioredis blob was present, just unreachable). Latent bug — surfaced after ioredis was added to apps/worker/package.json for the Redis-cache refactor.
bugs.fix: Added `COPY --from=build --chown=nodejs:nodejs /app/apps/worker/node_modules ./apps/worker/node_modules` to the Dockerfile runtime stage, with a long comment explaining the pnpm-isolated-mode + Docker pitfall so the next person doesn't strip it.

bugs.error_message: Runtime error at boot: `headers` was called outside a request scope (next-dynamic-api-wrong-context). Stack pointed at apps/web/middleware.ts:4 — `export default NextAuth(authConfig).auth()`.
bugs.root_cause: Next.js 16 + Turbopack bundles middleware.ts with the RSC path. Calling `NextAuth(authConfig).auth()` at module-evaluation time dispatches into Auth.js v5 code that calls `next/headers`'s `headers()` — which only works inside a request scope. Eager invocation = no request scope = crash. Must export the function reference (`.auth`, no parens) and let Next.js invoke it per-request.
bugs.fix: Changed `export default NextAuth(authConfig).auth();` to `export default NextAuth(authConfig).auth as NextMiddleware;`. Cast to `NextMiddleware` from `next/server` suppresses TS2742 — the inferred type of `.auth` can't be named without referencing next-auth's private `lib/` and `lib/types` subpaths. The runtime contract is unchanged; the cast is purely structural.
Rewrote 38→12 lines (27 removed) | Also: return normaliseTranscript(transcript);; const normaliseTranscript = (transcript: Transcrip

bugs.error_message: ReferenceError: Cannot access 'mockX' before initialization (vitest TDZ when vi.mock factory references top-level const mocks)
bugs.root_cause: vitest hoists vi.mock() factory calls to the top of the file before module-level const declarations are initialised. The factory closure references the mocks by name and hits a TDZ error at hoist time.
bugs.fix: Wrap mock declarations in `vi.hoisted(() => ({ ... }))` so they resolve before vi.mock factories run. Standard pattern: https://vitest.dev/api/vi.html#vi-hoisted

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Wrote apps/llm/llm-errors.ts by accident — file path was apps/<pkg>/src/lib/llm/ not apps/llm/. Caught on directory listing.
bugs.root_cause: Path mental model was wrong. The repo has apps/<pkg>/src/lib/<feature>/, not apps/<feature>/. The directory apps/llm/ at the workspace root doesn't exist as a convention — every feature lives inside an app.
bugs.fix: Moved apps/llm/llm-errors.ts → apps/worker/src/lib/llm/llm-errors.ts via mv, removed apps/llm/ directory. Going forward: double-check the destination directory with a quick `ls apps/<pkg>/src/lib/` before Write on a new sub-feature.

bugs.error_message: User-reported: 'the transation complete status is not showing over there.' Dashboard stayed on TRANSCRIBING badge after the worker flipped the row to GENERATING.
bugs.root_cause: SSE `STATUS_UPDATE` was the ONLY trigger for TanStack Query cache invalidation. If the SSE connection silently failed (Redis blip, dropped EventSource reconnection, channel-prefix drift between worker + API), the dashboard never refreshed and the row stayed on the old badge forever. `hasUnpublishedVideos` gating in `useVideoSSE` was also initially `videos.length > 0` — fine for the steady state, but transient SSE failures between renders had no recovery path.
bugs.fix: Added a 15 s safety-net polling refetch in DashboardContent, gated on `hasInFlightVideo` (any row in a non-final status: UPLOADED/EXTRACTING/TRANSCRIBING/GENERATING/READY/SCHEDULED/PUBLISHING/PUBLISH_FAILED/FAILED). The interval is free in the steady state — clears the moment no in-flight rows remain. Memoized the `videos` array via useMemo so the polling effect's dep array stays stable across renders.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: 'Cannot find package zod imported from apps/worker/src/lib/llm/schemas.ts'. schemas.test.ts + validate-with-retry.test.ts both fail to load.
bugs.root_cause: Worker package never declared zod as a direct dep — it was only available transitively through @clipflow/config. When schemas.ts started importing { z } from 'zod', pnpm's isolated-mode resolution couldn't find it under apps/worker/node_modules.
bugs.fix: Added `"zod": "^3.23.8"` to apps/worker/package.json dependencies (matching @clipflow/config's version). Following the pnpm + Docker pitfall in bug-048, also note: any direct dep added to a workspace package needs to land in apps/<pkg>/node_modules at container build time — the Dockerfile's runtime COPY already covers this for worker, so no additional change there.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: 'Argument of type undefined is not assignable to parameter of type Headers' on every OpenAI SDK error class constructor in llm-errors.test.ts.
bugs.root_cause: The openai SDK v6+ requires a `Headers` instance (not `undefined`) as the fourth constructor arg of every error subclass (AuthenticationError, NotFoundError, RateLimitError, etc.). The tests were passing `undefined` as a placeholder for 'no headers'.
bugs.fix: Added an `emptyHeaders(): Headers => new Headers()` helper at the top of llm-errors.test.ts, used as the final arg of every error construction. Tests don't care about the headers — only the status + error fields drive classifyLlmError.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: 'Type ChaptersJson is not assignable to type NullableJsonNullValueInput | InputJsonValue. Index signature for type string is missing in type ChaptersJson.'
bugs.root_cause: Prisma's InputJsonValue requires an explicit [key: string]: unknown index signature. The ChaptersJson interface declared `summary: string` + `chapters: {...}[]` without one, so the structural-typing check fails at the prisma.video.update call site.
bugs.fix: Cast at the call site: `chaptersJson: toChaptersJson(validated.output) as unknown as object`. Prisma serializes through JSON.stringify regardless of the structural type, so the cast is purely nominal — runtime behavior is identical. Kept the cast narrow (only at the persistence boundary) so the rest of the function still uses the typed ChaptersJson shape.

bugs.error_message: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'data'). In generate.test.ts, mapping mockPrismaVideoUpdate.mock.calls with `([, args]) => args.data?.status` returned [undefined, undefined] instead of the expected statuses.
bugs.root_cause: Off-by-one in mock.calls destructuring. vi.fn() records each call as an inner array — `prisma.video.update({ where, data })` is a single-arg call, so `mock.calls[0]` is `[{...}]`. `[, args]` skips the first element (which is the args object) and captures index 1 (always undefined for a single-arg call). The off-by-one only matters for filter/map — filter with a falsy predicate accidentally returns [] (test passes by luck), but map captures the wrong element and surfaces the bug.
bugs.fix: Changed `([, args])` → `([args])` everywhere it appears in the file. NOTE: the same off-by-one exists in transcription.test.ts:370 — the existing test passes because filter's predicate is false for undefined args.data, but it's a latent bug that should be fixed in a follow-up.
